content: {"pass": false, "issues": [{"problem": "No evidence ledger items are provided, so every substantive legal and commercial term (probation, leave, IP assignment, termination, benefits, etc.) is effectively unsupported by any cited source.", "fix": "Add ledger items or explicitly note that no external authorities or ADS fixed-default documents were consulted and that all terms are illustrative only."}, {"problem": "The SKILL checklist items about jurisdiction-specific rules (anti-indemnity, retainage, prompt payment, prevailing wage), flow-down obligations, NNN maintenance, and ratification for amendments/subleases are not addressed at all in the draft.", "fix": "Either add brief clauses/confirmations addressing each checklist item or explicitly state they are not applicable to this employment offer and mark them as N/A."}, {"problem": "The checklist requires \"Market-standard benchmarks applied\" but the draft does not reference any benchmark or indicate that benchmarks were considered.", "fix": "Add a short note that terms are intended to be broadly market-standard for Pakistan white-collar employment, subject to attorney confirmation."}, {"problem": "The checklist requires \"Flow-down obligations verified for subcontractor agreements\" but the draft does not state inapplicability.", "fix": "Add a sentence in a compliance or boilerplate section stating that subcontractor flow-down obligations are not applicable to this direct employment offer and mark as N/A for the checklist."}, {"problem": "The checklist requires an NNN maintenance matrix and structural repair allocation, which are not mentioned or marked as inapplicable.", "fix": "Explicitly state that NNN/structural repair concepts are not applicable to an employment offer and mark those checklist items as N/A."}, {"problem": "The checklist requires a ratification clause for amendments and subleases, which is not present; clause 10.2 only covers variations, not ratification.", "fix": "Add a brief ratification clause confirming that any prior understandings are superseded and that later written amendments will be ratified by both parties."}]}
